title: 山核桃大街谋杀案
source_file: data\raw\detectiveqa\novel_data_en\121-山核桃大街谋杀案-阿加莎克里斯蒂.txt
summary:
Hercule Poirot is unsettled when his highly efficient secretary, Miss Lemon, makes three mistakes in a simple letter. This uncharacteristic error leads Miss Lemon to reveal that she has been distracted by her sister. Miss Lemon explains that her sister, a widow who previously lived in Singapore, moved to England six months ago to work as a dormitory supervisor at a girls' boarding house located at 26 Walnut Street on Chestnut Street. The house is owned by a capricious woman of Greek descent named Mrs. Nikolai.

Miss Lemon describes her sister as a practical woman who is well-suited to managing the diverse, international student population. However, the sister has become increasingly anxious because items have been going missing from the dormitory in an unusual manner. Although Miss Lemon has not contacted the police, she is troubled because the thefts do not seem to follow ordinary explanations. Miss Lemon admits she lacks the imagination to speculate on the nature of the crimes, even after consulting medical literature.

Poirot, noting that Miss Lemon's distraction is affecting her work, decides to involve himself in the matter. While he tells himself he is only intervening to ensure his secretary returns to her perfect efficiency, he is secretly motivated by a sense of boredom and curiosity regarding the unusual thefts. He references a classic Sherlock Holmes line about celery and butter to signal his engagement with the mystery.
